content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/src/components/pipelineRuns-details/PipelineRunLogs.scss

Companion test files — you MUST read these after applying fixes:
- /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/src/components/pipelineRuns-details/__tests__/PipelineRunLogs.spec.tsx
After fixing the main file, read each test file above and check if any test:
- Opens a dropdown/menu/popover and queries for content (getByText, getByRole)
- Uses querySelector with OUIA selectors to interact with components
- Asserts on element roles, aria attributes, or data attributes that changed
If any test would break due to your changes, fix it. Common test fixes:
- Wrap assertions in waitFor() when content renders via portal
- Add popperProps={{ appendTo: 'inline' }} to component renders in tests
- Update getByRole/getByText queries to match new attribute values

Apply ALL of the following 4 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 12
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-log-viewer]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-log-viewer' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-log-viewer' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-log-viewer

Code context:
```
   11  
   12    .pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text {
   13      white-space: pre;

```

### Fix 2
Line: 12
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-log-viewer-text]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-log-viewer__text' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text

Code context:
```
   11  
   12    .pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text {
   13      white-space: pre;

```

### Fix 3
Line: 12
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-log-viewer]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-log-viewer' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-log-viewer' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-log-viewer

Code context:
```
   11  
   12    .pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text {
   13      white-space: pre;

```

### Fix 4
Line: 12
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-log-viewer-text]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-log-viewer__text' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text

Code context:
```
   11  
   12    .pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text {
   13      white-space: pre;

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/src/components/pipelineRuns-details/PipelineRunLogs.scss
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the SCSS file to understand its current content.
Now let me read the test file:

Now I have the full picture. Let me reason through the fixes:

**All 4 fixes target the same lines (11-14):** The `.pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text` selector on line 12. Fixes 1 & 3 say `pf-v5-c-log-viewer` is removed with no v6 equivalent. Fixes 2 & 4 say `pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text` is removed with no v6 equivalent. Since both the block class and element class have no v6 replacement, the entire rule block (lines 12-14) should be removed.

The test file doesn't reference any CSS classes, doesn't render components with dropdowns/menus/popovers, and doesn't use OUIA selectors or query for roles/aria attributes. It's purely a logic test. No test changes needed.
Let me verify the final result:

The file looks correct. The test file only tests logic (task run selection) and has no dependency on the removed CSS classes, so no test changes are needed.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-log-viewer` | ✅ Applied | Removed the `.pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text` rule block (lines 12-14) which contained the dead `pf-v5-c-log-viewer` class reference. No v6 equivalent exists. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-log-viewer-text` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — the `__text` BEM element class was part of the same deleted rule block. |
| Fix 3 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-log-viewer` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — duplicate rule targeting the same `pf-v5-c-log-viewer` class on the same line. |
| Fix 4 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-log-viewer-text` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — duplicate rule targeting the same `pf-v5-c-log-viewer__text` class on the same line. |

**Test file reviewed:** `PipelineRunLogs.spec.tsx` — no changes needed. The test file contains only logic tests for `getActiveTaskRun` (task run selection by label matching and failure status). It does not reference any CSS classes, render PatternFly components, or use DOM queries that would be affected by the CSS class removal.
